zh.Kendal, Westmorland, England: 英國維斯特莫藍德肯達爾
zh.Levens, Westmorland, England: 英國維斯特莫藍德列文斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當Matthew IV Redman於大約1330年出生在英國維斯特莫藍德列文斯時，他的父親Matthew III Redman41歲，母親Margaret Ireby25歲。他於大約1360年與Lucy結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1398年1月在英國越可爾哈伍德享年69歲。

zh-hans.Levens, Westmorland, England: 英国維斯特莫藍德列文斯
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Matthew IV Redman于大约1330年出生在英国維斯特莫藍德列文斯时，他的父亲Matthew III Redman41岁，母亲Margaret Ireby25岁。他于大约1360年与Lucy结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于1398年1月在英国越可爾哈伍德享年69岁。

nameMeanings.name: Redman
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English red (Old English hrēod) ‘reed’ + -man, presumably for a thatcher or reed-cutter. Compare Reeder .
English: in southeastern England sometimes a topographic name perhaps for someone who lived by a clearing, from Middle English rede (Old English rēod, rȳd) ‘clearing’ + man.
English: habitational name from Redmain (Cumberland), from Old English rēad ‘red’ + an uncertain second element, perhaps a dialect form of man ‘cairn’.

variants.name: Reeder
meanings: English: occupational name for someone who used reeds as a roofing material (a thatcher), a derivative of Middle English rede ‘reed’ + -er. Compare Reed and Redman .

English: topographic name for someone who lived by a clearing, a derivative of Middle English rede, ride, rude ‘clearing’ (see Reed ) + -er, synonymous and interchangeable with atte Rede, atte Rude ‘at the clearing’. This type of name, composed of a topographic term + -er, is common in Kent, Surrey and Sussex, and it is also found in Essex.
Dutch and German: variant of   Reder   3.

variants.name: Redhead
meanings: nickname for someone with red hair, from Middle English red ‘red’ + he(ve)d ‘head’. Compare Redman .
habitational name from an unidentified place in northern England called Redhead, probably meaning ‘red headland’.

variants.name: Rodman
meanings: English: occupational name for a thatcher or reed-cutter, from Southwest Middle English rod(e), rud(e) (Old English hrēod) ‘reed’ + -man. Synonymous with Redman 1.
English: topographic name for someone who lived at a clearing, from Middle English rode (Old English rodu) or Southwest Middle English rude (Old English rȳde) ‘clearing’ + man. See Redman 2.
Americanized form of German Rodmann: variant of Rodemann (see Rodeman ).

nameMeanings.name: Matthew
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name of the Christian evangelist, author of the first gospel in the New Testament. His name is a form of the Hebrew name Mattathia, meaning ‘gift of God’, which is fairly common in the Old Testament, being rendered in the Authorized Version in a number of different forms: Mattan(i)ah, Mattatha(h), Mattithiah, Mattathias, and so on. In the Authorized Version, the evangelist is regularly referred to as Matthew, while the apostle chosen to replace Judas Iscariot is distinguished as Matthias . A related name from the same Hebrew roots, but reversed, is Jonathan . Throughout the English-speaking world Matthew has been particularly popular since the 1970s.

nameMeanings.name: IV
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Matthew IV Redman was born about 1330, in Levens, Westmorland, England, his father, Matthew III Redman, was 41 and his mother, Margaret Ireby, was 25. He married Lucy about 1360. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died in January 1398, in Harwood, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 69.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Matthew IV Redman est né environ 1330, à Levens, Westmorland, Angleterre, son père, Matthew III Redman, avait 41 ans et sa mère, Margaret Ireby, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Lucy environ 1360. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé en janvier 1398, à Harwood, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 69 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Matthew IV Redman um 1330 in Levens, Westmorland, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Matthew III Redman 41 und seine Mutter Margaret Ireby 25. Er hat um 1360 Lucy geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb im Januar 1398 im Alter von 69 Jahren in Harwood, Yorkshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Matthew IV Redman è nato il circa 1330 nel Levens, Westmorland, England, il suo padre, Matthew III Redman, aveva 41 anni e il suo madre, Margaret Ireby, aveva 25 anni. Lui sposato Lucy circa 1360. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì nel gennaio 1398, nel Harwood, Yorkshire, England, all'età di 69 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Matthew IV Redmanが約1330年にイギリスウエストモーランドレベンスで生まれた時，父Matthew III Redmanは41歳，母Margaret Irebyは25歳であった。約1360年にLucyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は1398年1月にイギリスヨークシュアイアハーウッドで69歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Matthew IV Redman(이)가 대략 1330년 영국 웨스트모를랜드 레븐스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Matthew III Redman(은)는 41세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Ireby(은)는 25세였다. 그는 대략 1360년 Lucy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1398년 1월 영국 요르크실르 하우드에서 69세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Matthew IV Redman nasceu aproximadamente 1330, em Levens, Westmorland, Inglaterra, seu pai, Matthew III Redman, tinha 41 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Ireby, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Lucy aproximadamente 1360. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ele faleceu em janeiro de 1398, em Harwood, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, com 69 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Matthew IV Redman родился около 1330 г. в местности ливенс, вестморланд, Англия, его отцу по имени Matthew III Redman было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Margaret Ireby, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Lucy около 1360 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер январь 1398 г. в местности харвуд, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 69 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Matthew IV Redman nació aproximadamente 1330, en Levens, Westmorland, Inglaterra, su papá, Matthew III Redman, tenía 41 años y su mamá, Margaret Ireby, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Lucy aproximadamente 1360. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió en enero de 1398, en Harwood, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 69 años.
